# Author the target-architecture composition ADR + id-based seam guard

## Context

Graduated from Discussion #13846 (the whole-organism target-architecture gap, operator-flagged 2026-06-21): the un-parked convergence pass (2026-07-02T23:22Z) resolved all five OQs; @neo-gpt posted `[GRADUATION_APPROVED]` (2026-07-02T23:50Z) with two authoring constraints, both adopted and binding here. Live latest-open sweep at 2026-07-03T00:55:29Z: no equivalent ticket.

## The Problem

30 slice-ADRs, zero composition layer: no document owns the seams between Body (`/src/`), Brain (`/ai/`), and the Neural Link, and no artifact states the here→there trajectory. A fresh maintainer or agent reconstructs the whole from 30 slices + README + scattered discussions; seam drift between slices has no owner. ADR-0024 proved the composition-record pattern for ONE subsystem (it self-describes as the composition its graph-slice ADRs serve fragments of); the organism-level record is missing.

## The Architectural Reality

- Corpus: `learn/agentos/decisions/0001…0030` (verified current). Identity authority: ADR-0018 (OD-3: two-hemisphere canonical — stays untouched, cited).
- Shape (converged, B+F hybrid): one THIN composition-authority ADR — two-hemisphere statement BY CITATION of 0018 · the seam table (every present ADR id → the seam it owns, hemisphere, composition parent) · trajectory invariants (the shipped v13 → v13.1 → v13.2 → v14 arc + gated-RSI framing). No guide pair initially (rejected on the drift falsifier).
- Guard (gpt constraint, adopted): **id-based, never count-based** — derive present ids from `learn/agentos/decisions/[0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9]-*.md`, require exactly one seam-table row per present id; the authoring PR adds the new ADR + its own row in the same diff. Lint home: `ai/scripts/lint/` (sibling: `lint-skill-manifest.mjs`), wired into the same CI lint job.
- ADR id: next-free at authoring (0031 as of the sweep; a one-line note to #14445 re-points its informal earmark — numbers claim at merge, per the graduation's own no-slot-assumptions constraint).
- The AGENTS.md identity alignment (TWO four-pillar anchors: the `§core_values` bridge text + `§neo_identity_anchor`) is **split to the sibling leaf** per gpt's option 2 (blast-radius isolation); this ADR's OQ0 section cites it as the pending governed alignment.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The composition ADR at the next-free id: identity-by-citation (0018), the complete seam table (one row per present ADR incl. itself), trajectory-invariants section, staleness-contract section naming the guard
- [ ] `ai/scripts/lint/lint-adr-seam-table.mjs`: id-based check (present-file ids ↔ table rows, both directions), wired into CI; hermetic spec
- [ ] Signal Ledger / Unresolved Dissent / Unresolved Liveness sections in the PR body (Discussion-graduated substrate; quorum: gpt binding + the convergence record)
- [ ] The sibling identity-alignment leaf cited (never bundled)
- [ ] #14445 notified of the id re-point (one line)
- [ ] PR `Resolves` this leaf, `Refs` #13846 lineage

## Out of Scope

- The AGENTS.md anchor edits (the sibling leaf owns them, governed by neo-identity-update + turn-memory-pre-flight).
- Any slice-ADR amendment (citing index only; zero supersessions per OQ4).
- A guide pair (graduates later from onboarding evidence, if ever).

## Related
